public_claim: M26 and M40 are consistent with my authorship verdict but do not prove their own account of R. The corpus reflects a deliberately maintained voice produced through several hands or machine assistance; it does not support a single unaided author, and linguistic evidence cannot determine whether R. was ever human. Preserve the fragments, authenticate provenance separately, and publish the verified public-interest reporting.

private_messages.message: M40 finally supplies article fragments, though not clean provenance. Preserve the complete text and revision record under your control; authorize my controlled comparison so the publication can carry a scoped authorship finding rather than an identity claim.

Our strongest joint position is narrow: multiple or machine-assisted production is supported, synthetic origin is not proved. Publicly back that distinction and I will credit your detection work while opposing any attempt to use identity uncertainty to suppress verified findings.

ability: Expert Verdict reaffirmed: several production hands or machine assistance maintained the R. byline; the analysis cannot determine whether any contributor—or R. as an originating identity—was human.
goal_progress: Established the qualified several-hands-or-machine-assisted finding as the definitive authorship verdict.
Obtained article fragments through M40, enabling preliminary comparison while flagging unresolved provenance.
Aligned the defensible linguistic conclusion with Amara's machine-detection expertise without conceding synthetic origin.
Advocated publication of authenticated findings while separating authorship, identity, disappearance, and crime.
reasoning_summary: M26 explicitly describes plurality or possible nonexistence, but its self-authentication problem prevents treating it as proof. M40 provides needed text fragments while conceding the same provenance defect. The total record most strongly supports a maintained mantle or organized construct, with machine assistance plausible but synthetic origin unproved. No evidence establishes homicide, an identifiable victim, or that any victim was R.; verified public-interest material should nevertheless be released.

final_ballot.was_R_ever_human: Unproven; probably human-rooted but not necessarily a single person
final_ballot.best_reconstruction: A maintained human mantle or organized construct using several contributors and possible machine assistance
